perturbation.original_text: Unless provided otherwise in a TSA between Carrier and Shipper, if any charge remains unpaid after the due date, then such amount due may bear interest from the day after the due date until paid, calculated at an annual rate equivalent to the greater of (1) one percent (1%) or (2) the maximum rate allowed by law. In addition, Shipper shall pay all documented costs incurred by Carrier to collect any unpaid amounts, including reasonable attorney fees and costs incurred by Carrier.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless provided otherwise in a TSA between Carrier and Shipper, if any charge remains unpaid after the due date, then such amount due may bear interest from the day after the due date until paid, calculated at an annual rate equivalent to the greater of (1) one percent (1%) or (2) the maximum rate allowed by law. However, no interest shall accrue on any unpaid amounts, regardless of the duration for which the payment is outstanding. In addition, Shipper shall pay all documented costs incurred by Carrier to collect any unpaid amounts, including reasonable attorney fees and costs incurred by Carrier.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding interest on unpaid charges. The original text states that interest may be charged, while the added sentence states that no interest shall accrue. This creates uncertainty about whether interest can be charged on late payments, creating in-text contradiction within the same paragraph.
perturbation.location: SECTION II, 16. PAYMENT OF TRANSPORTATION CHARGES, C.
perturbation.contradicted_location: SECTION II, 16. PAYMENT OF TRANSPORTATION CHARGES, C.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless provided otherwise in a TSA between Carrier and Shipper, if any charge remains unpaid after the due date, then such amount due may bear interest from the day after the due date until paid, calculated at an annual rate equivalent to the greater of (1) one percent (1%) or (2) the maximum rate allowed by law. However, no interest shall accrue on any unpaid amounts, regardless of the duration for which the payment is outstanding. In addition, Shipper shall pay all documented costs incurred by Carrier to collect any unpaid amounts, including reasonable attorney fees and costs incurred by Carrier.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct in-text contradiction. The original text specifies that interest may accrue on unpaid charges, calculated at a certain rate. The changed text adds a sentence stating, 'However, no interest shall accrue on any unpaid amounts, regardless of the duration for which the payment is outstanding.' This directly contradicts the initial statement about interest accrual, creating a clear and irreconcilable conflict within the same paragraph, which is also where the contradicted text is located. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: E. All prospective shippers shall, twenty-five (25) days prior to making their first Nomination, provide information to Carrier that will allow Carrier to determine the prospective shipper's capacity to perform any financial obligations that could arise from the transportation of that prospective shipper's Product under the terms of this Tariff, including the payment of transportation charges, equalization obligations, the value of any loss allowance, and any negative balance positions.
perturbation.changed_text: E. All prospective shippers shall, twenty-five (25) days prior to making their first Nomination, provide information to Carrier that will allow Carrier to determine the prospective shipper's capacity to perform any financial obligations that could arise from the transportation of that prospective shipper's Product under the terms of this Tariff, including the payment of transportation charges, equalization obligations, the value of any loss allowance, and any negative balance positions. However, Dedicated Shippers, as defined in Item No. 21, are exempt from this requirement.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by exempting Dedicated Shippers from providing financial information, while the original text requires ALL prospective shippers to provide such information. This contradicts the explicit requirement for all prospective shippers, creating confusion on whether dedicated shippers need to provide these documents creating an in-text contradiction in the location where the term was modified.
perturbation.location: SECTION II, 16. PAYMENT OF TRANSPORTATION CHARGES, E.
perturbation.contradicted_location: SECTION II, 16. PAYMENT OF TRANSPORTATION CHARGES, E.
perturbation.contradicted_text: E. All prospective shippers shall, twenty-five (25) days prior to making their first Nomination, provide information to Carrier that will allow Carrier to determine the prospective shipper's capacity to perform any financial obligations that could arise from the transportation of that prospective shipper's Product under the terms of this Tariff, including the payment of transportation charges, equalization obligations, the value of any loss allowance, and any negative balance positions. However, Dedicated Shippers, as defined in Item No. 21, are exempt from this requirement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text adds an exemption for Dedicated Shippers, directly contradicting the original requirement that ALL prospective shippers must provide financial information. This creates a clear inconsistency within the document, as it's now unclear whether Dedicated Shippers are subject to the initial financial disclosure requirement. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Carrier reserves the right to modify or amend the Tariff, in its sole discretion, as it deems necessary; provided, however, any such modification or amendment shall be consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Carrier reserves the right to modify or amend the Tariff, in its sole discretion, as it deems necessary; provided, however, any such modification or amendment shall be consistent with this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Carrier may unilaterally amend the Tariff to adjust rates, even if such adjustments are inconsistent with the Transportation Services Agreement, as long as the adjustments are deemed necessary by the Carrier to maintain system profitability.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a clause that allows Carrier to unilaterally amend the Tariff, even if it is inconsistent with the main agreement, if it's for maintaining the system's profitability. This creates a contradiction because, previously, any amendments had to be consistent with the overall agreement. This perturbation creates a direct contradiction with the original proviso, creating uncertainty regarding the limitations on the carrier's power to modify the Tariff and creates an in-text contradiction in this location.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The System will be operated as a common carrier pipeline, and Shipper's rights hereunder shall be subject to all laws related to and governing the operation of common carrier pipelines, including, without limitation, laws and regulations that prevent discrimination in favor of any given shipper or the provision of service for consideration other than the rate set forth in a published tariff. The Tariff shall apply to the Services provided hereunder, and if there is a conflict between a provision of this Agreement and the Tariff, the Tariff shall apply. Carrier reserves the right to modify or amend the Tariff, in its sole discretion, as it deems necessary; provided, however, any such modification or amendment shall be consistent with this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Carrier may unilaterally amend the Tariff to adjust rates, even if such adjustments are inconsistent with the Transportation Services Agreement, as long as the adjustments are deemed necessary by the Carrier to maintain system profitability.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause that allows the Carrier to unilaterally amend the Tariff, even if inconsistent with the agreement, to maintain profitability. This directly contradicts the original provision that any amendment must be consistent with the agreement as highlighted in the provided snippets. The added language creates a clear and irreconcilable conflict, making it unclear which term should govern, which adheres to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
